assertions.text: Vehicle 1 was heading south in lane two of a two-lane protected turn lane.
The protected turn lane that Vehicle 1 occupied was about to merge into a south-bound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 2 was traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane, two-way road.
Vehicle 2 turned right to travel south into lane two of the south-bound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 1 made a right turn across a painted gore before the merge intending to enter a parking lot.
Vehicle 1's right side and Vehicle 2's front end made contact.
Both vehicles came to rest on the road.
There was a single contact between Vehicle 1 and Vehicle 2.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry belongs to vehicle V2; speed samples show [6.0 km/h first at -5.0 s and 28.0 km/h last at 0.0 s](T2) but there is no channel giving Vehicle 1's heading or lane assignment, so the claim about Vehicle 1 heading south in lane two is unverifiable.

No road geometry or lane-count metadata is present in the telemetry; EDR channels and summary for V2 ([max delta-V long = -17.0 km/h](T1)) do not confirm that Vehicle 1 was in a protected turn lane about to merge into a south-bound three-lane one-way road.

V2's telemetry shows vehicle speed ([6.0 km/h to 28.0 km/h](T2)) and crash pulse ([ -17.0 km/h long, 4.0 km/h lat ](T1)/(T2)) but contains no compass heading or lane/road-count metadata to establish that Vehicle 2 was traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane two-way road; therefore unverifiable.

The device-frame lateral delta-V ([4.0 km/h peak](T1)) and steering exist but without mapping to world heading or lane indices we cannot confirm that V2 turned right to travel south into lane two; therefore unverifiable.
The maneuver across a painted gore and intent to enter a parking lot are not observable in V2's EDR channels (speed, brake, delta-V), so the assertion is unverifiable.

V2 shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V ([ -17.0 km/h ](T2)) with a lateral component ([ 4.0 km/h ](T1)), but impact locations (V1 right side vs V2 front) are not encoded in these device-frame delta-V channels and axis-to-vehicle alignment is undocumented; thus unverifiable.

There are no post-impact speed samples to show final rest; pre-impact speed at [0.0 s] was [28.0 km/h](T2) and the crash pulse final cumulative delta-V is [ -17.0 km/h ](T2) but that does not by itself prove both vehicles came to rest on the road; therefore unverifiable.

The EDR/detection shows a single dominant longitudinal crash pulse with max cumulative delta-V [ -17.0 km/h ](T2) at [0.19 s](T2) and a single lateral delta-V peak [4.0 km/h](T1) at [0.07 s](T1); detect_events lists a single impact-delta_v detection for longitudinal and one for lateral (no multiple separated crash pulses), supporting that V2 experienced a single contact event (T3).

result.rationale: V2's telemetry clearly records a single crash pulse (max longitudinal delta-V [ -17.0 km/h ](T2) and lateral [ 4.0 km/h ](T1)), supporting a single contact for this vehicle; however, assertions about Vehicle 1's lanes, headings, maneuvers, and final resting positions require world-frame, lane, or partner-vehicle impact-location data not present in the provided channels and are therefore unverifiable.

citations.claim: 6.0 km/h first at -5.0 s and 28.0 km/h last at 0.0 s

uncited_quantitative_claims.context: pact speed samples to show final rest; pre-impact speed at [0.0 s] was   and the crash pulse final cumulative delta-V is   bu

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
